reviews.review_text:
Procrastinating in real life by cleaning and designing digital houses that I will never own in this economy. 🫠

10/10 would recommend.
Don't buy this game. You'll have to buy the remastered upgrade to fix everything wrong with it. Greedy devs just lost all future business from me.
Same gameply over and over. Controls not beginner friendly.

really good and fun game i just found myself running out of ideas and getting bored. But then i think to myself, what could this game add that would keep me entertained? i have no idea so i think this is a deeper problem within myself and this game helped me figure that out. Thanks house flipper

I absolutely love this game, I have over 2100+ hours over two accounts. All of my save data mysteriously vanished, FOR A THIRD TIME, without being able to be recovered. I cant find myself playing this game ever again without serious fixes.
second time ive loaded the game and its a complete lag - unable to play

they just released  the remaster version of the game when it pretty much the same game as this only updated graphics and higher price point being 50$ seems like this company now is focusing on cashing out and not optimizing or doing anything with this game anymore so if you want a boring sim to kill time wait till this one on sell do not buy the remaster version they cared about there player based they would gave this game a remaster dlc free to people who already bought there garbage

One of the most chill and satisfying games you'll ever play. Progression isn't too slow and there's plenty of furniture to decorate each house uniquely. Even more on the Workshop. 

My only downsides is that there's only a limited number of requests. I honestly prefer doing the requests more than the actual "buying house and flipping it" gameplay. Once I finish all the requests, I'm basically done with the game. 

That, and the NPC's are obsessed with saunas in this game for some reason (I assume devs are from Finland)

100% completed.

I bought [b]House Flipper[/b] thinking it would be a nice little game to pass some time, but I ended up finishing everything the game had to offer.

There's just something incredibly relaxing about cleaning up a complete mess, renovating a house and making it look good again. Whenever I wanted to chill, listen to music or watch something on a second monitor, this was the perfect game.

It's one of the coziest games I've played in a long time. No pressure, no complicated mechanics, just a satisfying gameplay loop that somehow never got boring for me.

Reaching 100% felt great, and honestly, I wish there was even more content left for me to do.

Definitely recommended if you want a game that helps you unwind and just enjoy the process. ❤️🏠
This game is a lot of fun. lots of different ways to make the houses look great. 8.3-10
This game is fun! It is like the decoration fun of sims and the building part with out having to keep up with sims that keep setting the house on fire..
love it, calming, relaxing but does get a little repetitive

I finished everything the base game has to offer in about 16.5 hours without any DLC. After that, it feels like it would just get repetitive and a bit dull doing the same things again, especially since the furniture options are pretty limited in my opinion.

The DLCs are also quite expensive, and I personally wouldn’t consider buying them. I still had a good time though, and for how cheap it can get on sale (down to 2€ or for free like last time), it’s definitely worth it for a few hours of fun.
great game hours pass quick while playing, just wish perk points were earned a little quicker, other wise two thumbs up recommended
They've recently released a remaster version and expect og owners to pay $45, ♥♥♥♥ that.

i didn’t expect to be so addicted to flipping houses but here we are… around 60 houses and having gotten all dlcs later, i can say that having dlc makes the game the most fun and liberating when it comes to what you can design and make, but even without dlc it is pretty fun. the controls are simple and the cleaning is satisfying.

House Flipper is a relaxing and surprisingly addictive game. If you’ve ever enjoyed decorating, renovating, or designing spaces, this game does a great job of letting you turn old, run-down houses into something completely different.

The gameplay loop is simple but satisfying. You start by cleaning, painting, repairing, and furnishing homes, then eventually buying properties to renovate and sell for a profit. Watching a messy house transform into a finished project never gets old.

There are plenty of customization options, and the freedom to design each house however you want adds a lot of replay value. While some tasks can become repetitive after a while, the constant progress and creativity keep things enjoyable.

Overall, House Flipper is a great game to relax with after a long day. Whether you’re into home design or just looking for a casual game to unwind, it’s definitely worth playing.
Super chill gameplay with lots of options for doing things your own way. Love the buy, renovate, sell mode over working for clients, much more scope for making changes.

Very relaxing game, allows you to freely work on tasks without feeling like you need to finish one section before another, great easter eggs hidden around, and a ton of customization options. if you love making houses in Sims then this is the game for you
This Is such a f****** perfect chill game, no stress and just your mind. IT'S PERFECT.
GOOD if you like to make things look nice and if you have bad OCD but it is a good game over all
It's pretty smooth and has some great graphics. The homes you visit get pretty neat. Lots of gameplay hours available.

I tear room out, put new room, life good. This is first person sims house building, I love it! I need to get the DLC's at some point, but the base game is plenty good all by itself, lots of options, lots of extras, lots of replayability! 

If things like Hozy, Sims building, and any house customisation in games is your thing, you will love this game :D

I am 16 hours in, sold one house, have 9 jobs left to play for the first time, decorated my office into my dream mini apartment, and am excited to go to sleep so i can play more tomorrow :) This is a good low cortisol, no stress, no problems, unwinding kind of game to enjoy after slaving away at work. Satisfying, rewarding, and worth the money. Have fun y'all!

Speaking as someone who loves the sims for build mode this is perfect. I love that i can access mods from the workshop and even have some of my fave sims 4 items in game. i love going ham on the clutter and decor. theres no empty book shelves around here!

Many more hours of play to come!

[h1] Handyman Simulator [/h1]

Very cool game, interesting mechanics, spent a lot of time simulating some work.

The problem is I went out of jobs, so I had to buy houses and work on them by myself and I don't have any creativity for that, so the game got very boring out of nowhere.

Other than that, cool stuff.

i love this game so much, i get so many ideas on remodeling the houses but randomly as i finished remodeling my bathroom, my whole room just turned into a shade i never seen before- not a harmful bug but never seen it before, 10/10 game though!

The cleaning and decorating portion of the game is relaxing. I absolutely enjoy that aspect! It's the selling portion that takes a lot of the fun out of it. You can't just decorate for your own taste, you have to set it up to please the clients and some of them have weird preferences. One guy doesn't want a kitchen. At all. Almost all of them want a sauna and with the early game houses there just isn't room for what a lot of them want! 

It's not a bad game but at the moment I regret not buying House Flipper 2 instead just to have more options. I'm also a little disappointed the jobs that come through email weren't spaced out a little. I focused on that as they piled up in the first few days I played but after finishing that initial burst there weren't any more so you can only make money buying and selling instead of getting that little extra cash with side jobs.
Easy to get absorbed in this game, I wouldnt call it "house flipper" - more like cleaning/fixing for other people that own a house
its really fun and creative i love this game recommend for those who want to build they dream house
Such an amazing game! Found this game while on sale and it has been then best purchase. Easy controls and could paly it for hours.
so fun! I could play this for literal hours, and, well, i have lol. super fun, engaging and addicting.
This Game Is Great Fun For Expirience, Knoladge and in genral great

I am on the fence on this one. It's got quie a few bugs and annoyances and just didn't hook me. I can see how certain people would love this game because you get to decorate your own home but I am not one of them and I just treated each room like a sort of Tetris game where I had to fit furniture in the least space possible.

The game is not meant for people who see games as things to be beaten. It's more for people who want to mess around decorating a house and making it look nice.

It's not an awful game so I've given it the benefit of the doubt.
Fun if you like interior design and cleaning things. The jobs have completionism satisfaction. Buying, modeling, and selling houses has design satisfaction.

i only got this game so i could bully my girlfriend more with mac from date everything. i made an entire shrine for them. 

this game's fun :D
Fun. I wish there were more jobs in the base game. I like having objectives, but there are houses you can buy and fix up to have more to do! Overall, a great game I'd play over again multiple times
What I like is this is a cozy type of game that helps with my anxiety. I really enjoy cleaning, remodeling and building. It keeps me busy when I have nothing else to do.

Absolutely love this game when I'm stressed out i just play this game to relax. i have it on thew Xbox and Nintendo as well as the computer i just love playing it that much lol. I love the details they are more in depth than what they use to be. It seems a little more easier to play as well though it seems a little glitchy of view when panning around still but that may just be my computer and I have changed the sensitivity around but still seems glitchy no matter where I put the sensitivity around at.

House Flipper is one of those games that is surprisingly hard to put down.

The gameplay is simple, relaxing and just feels good. Whether you're cleaning, painting, renovating, decorating or completely redesigning a house, the game always gives you something to do.

What keeps the game interesting is the amount of freedom you have. You can follow a specific style, recreate real houses or simply design whatever you like. With so many different properties and customization options, it rarely feels repetitive.

The DLCs make the experience even better by adding new locations, furniture, mechanics and houses to work on. And if you ever start getting bored, there's a good chance one of the expansions will give you plenty of new content to enjoy.

A relaxing and addictive game that I've spent many more hours in than I originally expected.

This game was well thought out and a really chill game to play if you want to relax and pass the time. I would recommend playing this as a pass time type of game. However, there is a bug where you lose your game save file. I wouldn't recommend getting this game until this gets fixed. Someone said to check the file properties and restart my pc. However, in the end that didn't end up working. Pretty sure the developer just needs to fix that themselves. Until then, don't buy this game till that issue is fixed, unless you're ok with losing your saved file and having to start from scratch...

I like the difference between this game and the mobile version. There are lots more things you can do. I love the characters and all the different things you can do to a home to make it better. I really like how you can buy, renovate, and sell houses as well as completing jobs for money.
Its super fun, if you want the satisfaction of cleaning and redecorating a room but dont have the energy or resources to do it irl, this the game for you. I lobe it. i lobe this game.
the game is nice but i wish it was a little bit more cozy. i mean we need a choice of being male/female,having relationship and pets! 4/5
Always a good time, would love a reality that reflects my game play. Thanks!

Honestly its just incredibly boring. There is not much space for creativity, the furniture/decor selection is incredibly limited, and the mechanics are nothing more than just holding down left click to complete tasks. I got it on sale for less than $3 and I DEFINITELY wouldn't recommend spending anymore than that on this. I'm honestly surprised it has such good reviews?
Even in 2026 i enjoy playing house flipper, its a relaxing game of creativity and i love it!

Base Game (with Xmas & Apocalypse DLC)

Great fun!

I like the missions more than the house flipping mode as those are more bite-sized. The little details that go into building these spaces are pretty amazing from someone who's used to The Sims. I never got this excited installing a water heater or an air-conditioning unit. Plastering is fun on this game and IRL lol.

It's only the small little things that really bug me... for example:
- I can't put any small decorative items outside.
- Can't mow the lawn wth?! I can make a damn good glass house with a front yard that belongs to a haunted house or something.

gdi Frozen District, you can have my money. brb buying DLCs sigh.

There are a lot of cosmetics and items to use in the flips. I do have all of the DLC and I haven't played the game without it, so I can't comment on the vanilla game, but I think it's a lot of fun! I like the buyers function although I wish there were more as some of the flips start to feel same-y, but there is still a lot of room to put different personalities on houses without going broke. The pets are the best part of the game tbh, whenever I load in the first thing I do is go check on them

from what i Personally Remember, This Game is truly good. i enjoyed it a l ot. it gots tons of things the animation when you brush is just amazing pretty chill for a simple game like that so final rating 11/10
If you actually thought that you'd flip a house, I'm sorry, but what are you thinking? The cleaning simulator that everyone needs to do in real life rather than in a game.
A very fun and relaxing game that you can get caught up in and spend hours playing
this game is oddly relaxing especially after a long day after work. Will recommend to anyone who likes structured building and cleaning.

Calm game.
Very addictive the first time you play.
But after playing through the "story" and buying and selling houses it's jover. You probably won't care to play it again, atleast not like the first time.
Placing small decorations is quite tedious especially if you're going for a realistic way of doing it withing cabinets and such.
I wish there were some npc interactions like you were actually meeting with clients and they explained their problems instead of an email and arriving to an empty house with a suspicious amount of boxes and somehow dirty sheets that you *mop* off.
This game is so cozy and I love it, truly a game of the ages...
Its a nice building game and a good game to play when you relax

this is a good game to play while listening to something in the background. 
the first bunch of houses were really fun as it was reallyyyy satisfying to start upgrading tools and such but the later houses just got kind of monotonous as it was mostly putting in furniture and that's my least favorite part of the game- but it's still really enjoyable no doubt.
If you love to decorate build and get that Satisfying feeling from a clean organized space than you will LOVE this game.  I do ;) Can be a little glitchy sometimes but worth it!!
a nice sandbox game to relax and decorate things as you like.
It's fun to renovate/ cleaning homes and easy to play. The only thing I don't like is the window cleaning part. That part is only easy when you aren't on a PC it's easy on the switch though.
Chill game if you're into interior designing/cleaning/mindless chores/making things look pretty

If you love to decorate, this is the game for you. I was more interested in renovations and installing appliances. Unfortunately the game weighs more towards decorating. The jobs made that simple, so I really enjoyed doing those while still being able to do the construction-ish things. Then the jobs ran out and now I'm left with houses to buy. Cleaning, renovating, and installing appliances for those houses are fun for me. Sadly to sell this houses you have to decorate to the tastes of these virtual strangers in game, which are very specific. Even if you give them what they want, they may still say you didn't do a good enough job. 

Performance-wise the game runs well enough, but I only have the base game currently. I don't know how well these will go if you play with all the DLCs installed. The forums mention a lot of bugs and performance issues for a game that has been out for so long, 4 years at the time of this review. 

I would only recommend this game, if you can get it deeply discounted or part of a bundle or free. I mean the DLCs too. Then you can play the way that you want and leave out anything you don't like.

I find this game relaxing and addicting. i have played for 154hrs have all the dlc's and cant wait for the remastered to come out!!! If your looking for a game to just clean, fix up , decorate at your own pace, then this game is for you. I have tried other games like this and none of them come close to the detail, game play and fun as this one!! This game is an A+++++ in my book!!!

This is a fun house design and decorating game. Sometimes it can feel a little redundant the way the houses are designed. I love busting down walls and creating my own space. I really enjoyed the DLC of the garden aspect of the game and love mowing. I also downloaded the restaurant addition to the game which gave some more missions.

A few things I'd like to be added:
* More missions
* More wall and floor designs
* Being able to items in a drawer (they just fall to the floor)
* Planting some tall trees like Birch trees that are usually present already but can't be replanted
* Creating stores
* More open world and traveling to neighbors' houses to find missions
* Driving a car around
* Seeing the fronts of some buildings
* Auto-filling cupboards with kitchen items

Overall, this is a really fun game and I'm looking forward to more DLC.

Although it's unlikely that I'll ever play House Flipper 1 again, since 2 is basically everything the first game was but better in many aspects, there's one key note that I want to highlight.

The music in this game is phenomenal. It has the same aura as The Sims 1, owing that to the similar instrument usage throughout, and ironically, much like Sims 1 to Sims 2, House Flipper 2 lost that charm along the way.

So if nothing else, if you won't play this game, you'll probably want to listen to this soundtrack over the sequel, because it's just that good.
Lots of fun and with dlcs it has over hours of new gameplay and I got someone else I know to play it and they also got the second game
Very relaxing. Controls are a little off at times, but not terrible.

Fun & simple game. I like to play it whenever I am not wanting to think all that hard but still play a game that I enjoy. Relatively easy controls, smooth gameplay, relaxing background music. Definitely recommend to others looking for a chill yet enjoyable game.
loved the game it was very therapeutic but it does get tiring after a while
the tile options are kinda limited but overall a good game without having buy a ton of dlcs

If you love cozy sim games, House Flipper is dangerously addictive in the best way possible. I got hooked almost immediately. There’s something super satisfying about cleaning, decorating, renovating, and slowly turning complete disasters into beautiful homes.
What I love most is how relaxing it is. There’s basically zero pressure, so it’s the perfect game to play while listening to a podcast, watching YouTube, or just unwinding after a long day. The gameplay loop is simple but weirdly rewarding, and it scratches that “just one more task” itch perfectly.
As someone who loves simulation games, this easily became one of my favorites. Cozy, satisfying, and incredibly easy to lose hours in. Highly recommend if you enjoy chill simulator games.
really fun but not great replay value. however, does scratch a specific itch every few years.

when i was growing up, i always wanted to play house flipper after seeing my fav youtubers like markiplier play it. now, i finally have a laptop (although, it sucks) it handles house flipper amazingly, even with the workshop addons, and i absolutely love it. the dlc's are definitely worth it in my opinion, especially the restaurant and luxury (dont come at me, they're just my favs). honestly, if you like to decorate, to renovate or to just chill and clean, please get this game because omg its so worth it. just wait till its on sale bc ive seen it at £20 once and believe me, as much as i love it, do NAT spend £20 on it. hope this helps x
House Flipper 🏚️ It's Good It's Great It's Amazing Its freaking House Flipper man come-on man But The game it's house flipper
it gets very very boring as if i actually got a job. i dont know why anyone would actually pay to work
So far I really like that it's very relaxed. It's a bit harder if you only have a touch pad, but I got the hang of it :D
Enjoyable and open enough to really play around with designs
REALLY fun and interactive. there's something soothing about cleaning up and decorating
Controls are very easy to know and learn, and it is very satisfying to see a house go from dirty and missing a whole bunch of things to looking as if it is a brand new house.

[h2]From Disaster Zones to Dream Homes[/h2]
[h3]Rating: 9/10[/h3]

If you told me I’d spend my weekend virtually scrubbing floorboards and painting walls instead of doing it in real life, I’d have called you crazy. But House Flipper proves that there is a deep, primal satisfaction in taking something broken and making it beautiful. It’s addictive, relaxing, and surprisingly deep.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3548314092
The progression from cleaning up trash to knocking down walls and installing radiators is incredibly rewarding. There’s a specific "zen" to the gameplay; you put on a podcast or some music, and suddenly it’s 3 AM, and you’ve just finished a luxury kitchen/bathroom. The loop of buying a "dump," fixing it up, and seeing the profit roll in at the auction is a masterclass in satisfying game design.

While I loved almost every minute, there are areas where it could be improved. The buyer's requirements are quite odd to guess at, especially when everyone required something different. You have to remember what you have completed and what is left; if the game tracked it, that would make it 10/10. The furniture selection in the base game can start to feel a bit repetitive after a dozen houses, and the placement mechanics can occasionally be a bit finicky when you're trying to get things perfectly symmetrical. 
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3443292059
The Apocalypse DLC adds a unique survival twist to an already great game. If you love decorating or prepping for the end of the world, this is a must-play.

[h3]Time?[/h3]
Took me 48 hours to complete the Base game +  The Apocalypse DLC. 
Someday, I'll grab the other 7 DLCs, complete those, and update the time here.
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3440258087
[h3]Final Verdict[/h3]
Brilliant, satisfying, and deeply relaxing. A near-perfect Sim for anyone who loves a good 'before and after'. Looking forward to jumping into HF 2.

[i]Leave a Like if you found this review useful[/i]

removed all the walls of a house i bought, plastered the inside and outside with bathroom tiles and placed a single toilet in the middle with a spotlight. The only entrance to this beautifull abode I call my home now is through a garage door.

Frozen Way has openly lied to players about the GenAI images present in later DLCs. Go to the Party Furniture Pack, go to the third screenshot, and you'll see two GenAI posters. Isolate and reverse image search them, and you'll find those posters for sale on dozens of websites, sometimes grouped together with a third.

It's possible the GenAI content is merely a fault in diligence when picking out stock assets. However; Frozen Way, in a developer response to someone who pointed out GenAI images in a Dine Out DLC review, said that "all of our art is handcrafted by our dedicated artists". Even if, for some reason, you don't care about the presence of GenAI assets in a game, Frozen Way's dishonesty should be concerning.

I can't meaningfully recommend this product when the company behind it has no qualms about lying to its customers.
